sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent pain in his left hand, which has been fluctuating over the past few days. He reports that the pain was severe last week but has improved somewhat. He also reports occasional worrying thoughts and difficulty sleeping, sometimes staying awake due to these concerns. He reports taking his metformin daily and has been taking his blood pressure readings at home, noting readings of approximately 130/80 to 140/90. He recalls receiving percocet for pain relief last week. He reports that he has been having trouble giving up fast food and cakes and cookies.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure was measured at 150/90. Heart rate is 78 bpm and regular. Neurological exam is unremarkable. The patient's A1C was 6.7%.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a history of hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and recurrent kidney stones. He is currently managing his hypertension with norvasc and his diabetes with metformin.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a referral to urology. Order a urinalysis. Order ten milligrams of melatonin as needed. Continue metformin at 500 mg daily. Continue norvasc at 2.5 mg daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in three months. Recommend practicing proper sleep hygiene, including turning off electronics and ensuring a dark room.
